name: haldir
summary: Scoped sessions, encrypted vault, immutable + tamper-evident audit log, RFC 6962 Merkle proofs, and SOC2 audit-prep evidence packs for AI agents.
description:
Haldir is the governance layer agents connect to before calling tools. Use it to: mint scoped sessions with spend limits and TTLs (Gate); store and retrieve encrypted secrets with AES-256-GCM + AAD binding (Vault); log every tool call to a SHA-256 hash-chained + RFC 6962 Merkle-signed audit trail with anomaly detection (Watch); request human-in-the-loop approvals; generate signed audit-prep evidence packs for SOC2 workpapers; and proxy MCP tool calls through a policy enforcement layer.

The tamper-evidence story is cryptographic, not monitoring: every audit log has a Signed Tree Head an auditor can pin, and inclusion/consistency proofs verify offline with the same primitive Certificate Transparency uses for the global WebPKI.

authentication.instructions: Pass an API key via `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Mint the first key with `POST /v1/keys` (no auth for the first key) or `haldir init` via the CLI.

use_cases.name: Scope + spend-limit an agent before it touches tools
Log a tool call so the chain + Merkle tree cover it
Prove an audit entry is in the current signed tree
use_cases.example: get_inclusion_proof(entry_id='ent_xxx')  # returns RFC 6962 audit_path + root + STH

use_cases.name: Prove the current tree is an append-only extension of an earlier STH
Generate signed audit-prep evidence for a SOC2 workpaper

trust_signals.asymmetric_sth_signing: Ed25519 via /.well-known/jwks.json (opt-in when HALDIR_TREE_SIGNING_KEY_ED25519_SEED is set)
